description: This figure presents a CALPHAD-calculated property diagram showing equilibrium phase amounts versus temperature for the low-density Fe40Mn19Ni15Al15Si10C1 high entropy steel. The horizontal axis spans approximately 500–1500 K and the vertical axis indicates the amount of phases from 0 to 1.2 mol. Six phase fields are plotted: BCC_A2(FE,MN;VA) in red, B2_VACANCY(AL,NI) in blue, LIQUID(FE,MN) in purple, M3SI(MN,FE:SI) in olive, BCC_A2#3(FE;VA) in yellow, and BCC_A2#2(MN;VA,C) in green. At low temperatures, the alloy comprises multiple solid phases including B2 at roughly 0.3 mol, BCC_A2, M3Si silicide, and minor BCC_A2 variants. The M3Si and BCC_A2#3 phases vanish below 600 K, after which BCC_A2 becomes dominant and stabilizes near 0.7 mol between 800 K and 1200 K. The B2 phase decreases gradually with increasing temperature. BCC_A2#2 reappears discontinuously at a trace level near 1100 K. Liquid phase formation initiates near 1200 K and rises sharply, reaching 1.0 mol by approximately 1450 K where all solid phases fully dissolve, indicating complete melting. The diagram illustrates the predicted thermal stability, phase succession, and melting behavior of the alloy.
